# How to patch a package

Method 1: Use patch-package
patch-package is a tool that lets you patch dependencies directly in your node_modules after installing them. It works well for scenarios where you need to make small modifications to third-party packages without forking or republishing them.

Steps to Use patch-package:
Install patch-package:

bash
Copy code
### npm install patch-package
Install the package you want to modify: For example, install d3-dsv:

bash
Copy code
### npm install d3-dsv
Modify the installed package: Go to your node_modules/d3-dsv directory and modify the objectConverter function or any other part of the code you want to change.

Create the patch: After modifying the package, run patch-package to create a patch file:

bash
Copy code
### npx patch-package d3-dsv
Commit the patch: The patch will be saved in the patches directory of your project. You can commit this directory to your version control system (e.g., Git):

bash
Copy code
### git add patches
### git commit -m "Patch d3-dsv to replace objectConverter"
Apply the patch automatically on npm install: When someone installs your project dependencies (or if you reinstall), the patch will be automatically applied.

Example:
Suppose you want to change the objectConverter function inside d3-dsv. You would:

Modify the function in node_modules/d3-dsv/index.js:

javascript
### Copy code
    function objectConverter(columns) {
        return function(d) {
            return Object.fromEntries(columns.map(function (name, i) { return [name, d[i]] }));
        }
    }
### Run npx patch-package d3-dsv to create the patch.

The patch file will look something like this:

bash
Copy code
patches/d3-dsv+1.2.0.patch
And will contain the code diff of the changes you made.

Add this in your package.json.
json
## Copy code
{
  "scripts": {
    "postinstall": "patch-package"
  }
}

Now, when you or anyone else runs npm install, the patch will automatically be applied to d3-dsv.
